title: Laccosperma opacum
taxonomy: Laccosperma opacum is a species of palm tree in the family Arecaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is commonly found in the savannas and grasslands of the region. The classification of Laccosperma opacum is further divided into the genus Laccosperma and the tribe Cocoeae, which is a subfamily of palms. The palm is characterized by its slender stem and pinnate leaves, which are a distinctive feature of the species. Within the Arecaceae family, Laccosperma opacum is part of the larger group of palms that are widely distributed across tropical and subtropical regions.
